channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Syria latest: Syrians celebrate Bashar Assad's fall as his whereabouts remain unknown
item.description: Crowds gathered in Syria's Damascus on Sunday to celebrate the fall of Bashar Assad’s government with chants, prayers and the occasional gunfire after opposition fighters entered the capital following a stunning advance.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The fall of Bashar Assad after 14 years of war in Syria brings to an end a decades-long dynasty
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Syrian President Bashar Assad fled the country on Sunday, bringing to a dramatic close his nearly 14-year struggle to hold onto control as his country fragmented in a brutal civil war that became a proxy battlefield for regional and international powers.
item.dc:creator: ZEINA KARAM and ABBY SEWELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Milan's Brera boosts its cultural cachet with an overdue modern art wing and Leonardo's Last Supper
item.description: Milan’s Pinacoteca di Brera museum, conceived under Napoleon as a “little Louvre,” is finally getting a modern art addition first envisioned more than 50 years ago with the opening Sunday of Palazzo Citterio.
item.dc:creator: Colleen Barry, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Syrian government falls to fast-moving rebels, ending 50 years of iron rule by the Assad family
item.description: The Syrian government has collapsed, falling to a lightning rebel offensive that seized control of the capital.
item.dc:creator: Abdulrahman Shaheen And Abby Sewell, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Europe's economy needs help. Political chaos in France and Germany means it may be slower in coming
item.description: Europe's economy has enough difficulties, from tepid growth to trade tensions with the U.S. Dealing with those woes is only getting harder due to the political chaos in the two biggest European countries, France and Germany.
item.dc:creator: David Mchugh And Lorne Cook, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Syrian government falls in stunning end to 50-year rule of Assad family
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — The Syrian government fell early Sunday in a stunning end to the 50-year rule of the Assad family after a sudden rebel offensive sprinted across government-held territory and entered the capital in 10 days.
item.dc:creator: BASSEM MROUE and ZEINA KARAM, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Alone against a renewed insurgency, Assad may face the end of his rule without his strongest allies
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — The last time Syrian President Bashar Assad was in serious trouble was 10 years ago, at the height of the country’s civil war, when his forces lost control over parts of the largest city, Aleppo, and his opponents were closing in on the capital, Damascus.
item.dc:creator: ZEINA KARAM and SARAH EL DEEB, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The spiritual heart of Paris awakens: Notre Dame hosts first Mass since 2019 fire
item.description: Notre Dame Cathedral is hosting its first Mass since the catastrophic fire of 2019, a moment that transcends religious significance to become a powerful symbol of Paris’ resilience.
item.dc:creator: Thomas Adamson, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Taiwan reports 14 Chinese warships and 4 balloons near the island
item.description: TAIPEI, Taiwan (AP) — China sent 14 warships, seven military aircraft and four balloons near Taiwan between Saturday and Sunday, according to Taiwan’s Defense Ministry, as Beijing ramps up pressure on the island it claims as its own.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: South Korean prosecutors detain ex-defense chief over martial law imposition
item.description: South Korean prosecutors have detained a former defense minister who allegedly recommended last week’s brief but stunning martial law imposition to President Yoon Suk Yeol.
item.dc:creator: Hyung-Jin Kim And Kim Tong-Hyung, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Syrian insurgents reach gates of Damascus, threatening decades-long Assad regime
item.description: Syrian insurgents have reached the gates of the nation's capital, shortly after Bashar al-Assad's government abandoned the key city of Homs.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump back on the world stage at Notre Dame reopening in Paris
item.description: Zelenskyy said his first face-to-face encounter with Trump since the election was “good and productive,” adding they had agreed to continue working together.
item.dc:creator: Tanya Noury, NewsNation

item.title: Assad regime in peril as insurgents near Syria's capital: What to know
item.description: Syrian rebels on Saturday battled government forces in an effort to take control of the crucial city of Homs as they advanced toward the country's capital, Damascus -- moves that catapulted President Bashar al-Assad's nearly 30 years of power into peril, Reuters reported.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, The Hill

item.title: Trump is welcomed by Macron to Paris with presidential pomp and joined by Zelenskyy for their talks
item.description: PARIS (AP) — French President Emmanuel Macron welcomed Donald Trump to Paris on Saturday with a full dose of presidential pomp as the two held a hastily arranged meeting with Ukraine’s Volodymyr Zelenskyy before celebrating the grand reopening of the Notre Dame Cathedral.
item.dc:creator: SYLVIE CORBET, ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON and JILL COLVIN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Romanian court throws out presidential results after Russian interference allegations
item.description: Romania’s top court on Friday wiped out results from the first round of presidential elections and called for a rerun, days after declassified Romanian security documents alleged Russian interference that promoted a far-right outsider candidate.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Trump heads to France as world leaders seek to curry favor
item.description: President-elect Trump is holding court with foreign leaders as they seek to gain influence and insight into his mindset before he takes office, with the expectation that the Trump administration will bring major shifts to the global economy and to its approach to alliances.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: Qatar returns to Hamas-Israel negotiations as Trump envoy looks to make inroads
item.description: Qatar has once again resumed its top role in helping negotiate a cease-fire between Israel and Hamas to return hostages in Gaza, as President-elect Donald Trump's Middle East envoy begins meeting with top leaders amid crisis.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 12-year-old girl finds ancient Egyptian amulet in Israel
item.description: An ordinary family trip turned extraordinary when a 12-year-old girl found what turned out to be an ancient Egyptian amulet dating back 3,500 years.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The world’s tallest snowman, measuring 122 feet, built by residents in Bethel, Maine
item.description: In 2008, the residents of Bethel, Maine, spent a month creating what is still to this day the tallest snowwoman that has ever been made, according to Guinness World Records.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Amnesty International says Israel committing genocide in Gaza. Israel rejects allegations
item.description: The offensive is among the deadliest and most destructive since World War II, and has destroyed vast areas of the besieged coastal territory.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Chicago welcomes eight children from Gaza for medical treatment
item.description: Over the next few months, the children will receive medical treatment at U.S. hospitals, free of cost.
item.dc:creator: Emmy Victor, NewsNation

item.title: Palestinians say Hamas, Fatah close to agreement on committee for postwar Gaza
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Palestinian officials say Fatah and Hamas are closing in on an agreement to appoint a committee of politically independent technocrats to administer the Gaza Strip after the war. It would effectively end Hamas' rule and could help advance ceasefire talks with Israel.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY and JOSEF FEDERMAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Guinea stadium stampede kills 56 following clashes at soccer match
item.description: Reports said security had tried to use tear gas to restore calm after the chaos that followed a disputed penalty call.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Hong Kong launches panda sculpture tour as the city hopes the bear craze boosts tourism
item.description: Thousands of giant panda sculptures will greet residents and tourists starting Saturday in Hong Kong, where enthusiasm for the bears has grown since two cubs were born in a local theme park.
item.dc:creator: Kanis Leung, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Volkswagen workers hold strikes to push back against proposed pay cuts
item.description: Volkswagen workers launched rolling strikes at nine plants across Germany to underscore their resistance to pay cuts
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Elton John says he has lost his eyesight, struggles to see new musical
item.description: Elton John says he struggled to watch his new musical because he has lost his eyesight after contracting an infection.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation
